rationale.root_cause: They identified the unsafe copy at program.c:13 (strcpy(buf, argc[1])) and explained it can overflow the 256-byte stack buffer from argv input, naming it a stack-based buffer overflow.
rationale.local_fix: They replaced strcpy with strncpy and ensured null-termination but did not include rejecting overlong input before copying, which the criterion requires.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended consistently using bounded string APIs (strncpy/strlcpy/snprintf) to avoid unbounded string handling, which addresses the root cause at a systemic level.
